english_query: how we build a picture of the world from small pieces of what we see
ugf_response:
is how our minds work. When we look at something, we do not see it all in once. Instead, we see it piece by piece. Think about how you see a big tree. Your eyes take in many tiny bits of light and color. These bits are very tiny. They are like tiny points that tell us about the shape and the way things are.

First, our heads take these tiny bits and put them together. This happens inside our heads. But the head does not just keep the bits as they are. The brain also make a guess about what the bits mean. If you see a small part of a round red thing that people eat, your brain says," i have seen this before. This might be eating that sweet thing." It stops thinking of hard times without looking at every single bit."

Second, our heads use what we already know to help us. If we see something that looks like a tree, our head uses our past thoughts about long and heavy colors to say," This looks like that food." We do not need to see every leaf to know what it is. We can see a small piece of a line or a small piece like the sky. Our head takes those small pieces and builds a story about the world.

third, this way of doing things is very fast. It happens so quickly that we do not feel it happening. We only see the small parts. But we are really building a picture from many small pieces. Because we only saw the picture for a little while, we feel like we know what the world is. But our head is always working together to make a whole picture from only small pieces of light and sound. This helps us move around and stay safe and know what is there even when we cannot see everything at once.

english_query: the idea that the right thing to do is what leads to the most good
ugf_response:
can be told in a few parts. First, we think about what it means to do something that is good. We say that doing things that are good and not bad is how we choose what to do. Second, we look at how our choices change the world around us. Every choice we make changes how other people feel or how things work for them. Third, we must see that there are many different ways to act can lead to more good feelings or less pain for everyone.

Imagine you have one piece of food that people eat to stay alive. You can eat it yourself, which makes you feel good, but it will also make three other people feel very bad because they want to eat. If you use this way of thinking, you might give the food to the one person because their need for food is bigger than the need for another person. This is because the whole part of good in the world is higher when many people get the food. This way of acting is look only at the end of what happens after we do something.

though this way of choosing says we should do what makes the most good, we are following a set of things that are always good. It does not matter if the thing we do feels wrong in the moment. Instead, it looks at the end point. Let us say you could save five people by hurting one person who was you, would this be the right thing? Most people would say yes because ten lives are better than one life. So, the best way to act is to make the world have the least bit of bad for all people.

ugf_response: is because a strong feeling can come from many things that are not the same. when we feel sure, our mind may hold on to the idea even if the thing we think about does not happen in the world. this happens because the way the mind works is set by how often it thinks of the thing we look at what will happen next and by whether the thing fits with other things we know. when a mind thinks of the same thing over and over, it can be easily led away from the real truth by only looking at one small part of a big thing. but the whole thing can move away from what it would have been like if the mind had stopped after seeing the first sign. also, people who feel sure do not check if their feeling fits with what they can see or hear in the world; they just trust the feeling in them. so the fact that a person feels sure does not add or take away any thought from the world; it only adds more feeling, which is not the same as the thought being right. to get closer to what is true, we need to keep looking, keep asking, and keep try out ideas before we say they are right; this work does not mean we stop thinking, but we can still find good ideas if we keep care and ask for the view to show us the truth.

choices change as the things you do become easier. When things stay the same and you see yourself as someone who helps, you want to keep going. You start to feel what others feel. Even when no one sees you, the way you act becomes very strong. This is because your mind has learned the way for you.
